provenance.source_corpus: Effects of substrate availability and competing vegetation on natural regeneration of white spruce on logged boreal mixedwood sites

source_spans.text: Feather moss, thick soil surface organic layers, litter, and solid wood were poor substrates for establishment.
Feather moss, thick soil surface organic layers, litter, and solid wood were poor substrates for establishment.
Feather moss, thick soil surface organic layers, litter, and solid wood were poor substrates for establishment.
Feather moss, thick soil surface organic layers, litter, and solid wood were poor substrates for establishment.
Early successional mosses establishing on mineral soil, thin organics, and rotten wood were generally favourable microsites
In Peace River, exposed mineral soil was the most strongly preferred substrate.
Early successional mosses establishing on mineral soil, thin organics, and rotten wood were generally favourable microsites
Early successional mosses establishing on mineral soil, thin organics, and rotten wood were generally favourable microsites
On the ripper-ploughed conifer-dominated sites, the shelf position... resulted in the highest early seedling establishment of white spruce
On the ripper-ploughed conifer-dominated sites, the shelf position... and scraped rotten wood resulted in the highest early seedling establishment of white spruce
Burial by aspen litter greatly increased seedling mortality
Burial by aspen litter greatly increased seedling mortality, especially when combined with a brief period of submergence
Both temporary spring flooding and especially cover by aspen leaf litter in the spring reduced seedling survival
heavy competing vegetation had a negative impact on older seedlings
There was some evidence that survival and growth of seedlings were improved by surrounding vegetation in the first years
In Peace River... all four measures of sapling (primarily aspen suckers) abundance were higher in plots in which seedlings survived
live graminoid cover was negatively associated with seedling survival
In Grande Prairie, forb abundance... was higher in plots in which seedlings survived
cover of tall saplings was negatively related to growth
establishment of white spruce
survival and growth of seedlings
survival and growth of seedlings
seedling mortality
